claims.claim: Together: humans respond to cues in ~300ms; >500ms you notice; 1-2s people hang up
claims.found: raw transcript: 'we respond to each other's cues in something like 300 milliseconds... taking more than 500 milliseconds to respond, you'll start to notice. If it takes a second, if it takes 2 seconds, people will just hang up.'

claims.claim: Vapi: conversational flow breaks when latency exceeds ~1200ms, described as time for user to have a tangential thought
claims.found: 'Conversational flow breaks when latency exceeds ~1200ms ("the rough time it takes for the user to have a tangential thought").'

claims.claim: Vapi hard-codes 1200ms latency budget per turn
Vapi: budget allocation - save ms in LLM reasoning -> spend on higher-fidelity TTS
claims.found: 'Treat budget as scarce resource: save ms in LLM reasoning -> spend on higher-fidelity TTS.'

claims.claim: Modal: natural-conversation floor can be as low as 100ms v2v
claims.found: 'Natural conversation v2v latency can be as short as 100ms.'

claims.claim: Modal: apps realistically target ~1s or less
Modal benchmarks against proprietary services' median (~1s on par with proprietary)
claims.found: 'median v2v latency of ~1 second, on par with proprietary services.'

summary: All 7 source figures verified against ground truth. together-talk numbers (300/500/1-2s) confirmed in raw YouTube JSON transcript verbatim. Vapi 1200ms budget + tangential-thought framing + TTS-tradeoff confirmed. Modal 100ms floor, ~1s target, and proprietary-median benchmark confirmed. No code_refs to check (frontmatter empty). No WRONG or MISCITED claims.
